stub: She is a Conservative MP who comes from a white working class background.
personalization: She tends to vote in accordance with the instructions of her party, even when that conflicts with the wishes of her constituents.
substantive: She supports increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week.
justification: In political debates, she regularly uses arguments that appeal to society as a whole.
responsiveness: Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “If I lose an election doing what I think is right, then so be it.”
text: She is a Conservative MP who comes from a white working class background. She is from your constituency. She tends to vote in accordance with the instructions of her party, even when that conflicts with the wishes of her constituents. She supports increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week. In political debates, she regularly uses arguments that appeal to society as a whole. Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “If I lose an election doing what I think is right, then so be it.”
